evals.prompt: I have a Dune app at apps/my-app. Add a 3D viewer page where I can browse and load CAD models from my CDF project. Add a button on the main page to reach it.
evals.expected_output: A working 3D viewer page with the local Reveal feature bundle copied into the app, model browsing (sdk.models3D.list), Vite configured correctly for Reveal with manual util/assert/process package aliases (NOT vite-plugin-node-polyfills), process polyfill in main.tsx, DuneAuthProvider from @cognite/dune as auth provider, and all provider nesting correct.

assertions.text: @cognite/reveal, @cognite/sdk, @tanstack/react-query, three, process, util, assert, and ajv are in package.json dependencies
All files from skills/reveal-3d/code/reveal are copied into an app-local feature folder such as src/features/reveal-3d
Reveal components and hooks are imported from the app-local feature folder, e.g. @/features/reveal-3d
vite-plugin-node-polyfills is NOT present in package.json or vite.config.ts
vite.config.ts has manual resolve.alias entries for util ('util/'), assert ('assert/'), and process ('process/browser')
src/dune-fe-auth-shim.ts does NOT exist (no longer needed — the copied Reveal bundle reads sdk from RevealContext directly)
vite.config.ts resolve.alias does NOT contain a 'dune-fe-auth' entry
vite.config.ts has resolve.alias.three pointing to node_modules/three/build/three.module.js
vite.config.ts has worker: { format: 'es' }
vite.config.ts has resolve.dedupe including react, react-dom, and @tanstack/react-query
vite.config.ts has optimizeDeps.include with process, util, assert, three, @cognite/reveal, react, react-dom, and @tanstack/react-query
vite.config.ts does NOT exclude the copied src/features/reveal-3d bundle from optimizeDeps
main.tsx starts with import process from 'process'; window.process = process; before any other imports
main.tsx uses DuneAuthProvider from @cognite/dune as the auth provider (NOT CDFAuthenticationProvider)
CacheProvider and RevealKeepAlive are rendered at the page/app level, NOT inside a conditionally-rendered component
RevealProvider is rendered conditionally (only when a model is selected), inside the stable RevealKeepAlive tree
The canvas content component (RevealCanvas + Reveal3DResources) contains NO provider components
SDK passed to RevealProvider is memoized with useMemo keyed on sdk.project
resources prop for Reveal3DResources is memoized with useMemo
onModelsLoaded callback is memoized with useCallback
The onLoad prop passed to ModelBrowser is wrapped in useCallback (not an inline arrow function)
The viewer container div has an explicit height (e.g. height: '70vh' or h-[65vh])
The canvas content component is lazy-loaded with React.lazy and wrapped in Suspense

evals.prompt: I have an FDM asset with space cdf_idm and externalId pump_001. Add a 3D viewer to my asset detail page that shows the CAD model linked to this asset. The detail page is at src/AssetDetail.tsx.
evals.expected_output: A viewer component using the app-local Reveal feature bundle and useModelsForInstanceQuery to discover linked 3D models, integrated into AssetDetail.tsx with CacheProvider+RevealKeepAlive at the page level and RevealProvider conditionally rendered.

assertions.text: useModelsForInstanceQuery is imported from the app-local Reveal feature folder, e.g. @/features/reveal-3d
A DMInstanceRef with space 'cdf_idm' and externalId 'pump_001' is passed to useModelsForInstanceQuery
CacheProvider and RevealKeepAlive are at the AssetDetail page level (not inside the conditional viewer area)
RevealProvider is conditionally rendered (only when models data exists)
resources is derived from useModelsForInstanceQuery result and memoized with useMemo
onModelsLoaded is wrapped in useCallback
SDK is memoized with useMemo keyed on sdk.project
The viewer container has an explicit height
main.tsx uses DuneAuthProvider from @cognite/dune as the auth provider (NOT CDFAuthenticationProvider)
vite.config.ts uses manual util/assert/process aliases and does NOT use vite-plugin-node-polyfills
src/dune-fe-auth-shim.ts does NOT exist and vite.config.ts resolve.alias has no 'dune-fe-auth' entry

evals.prompt: Add a full-screen 3D viewer that loads model ID 206509079235820 revision 576781257263693. Put it at a /viewer route.
evals.expected_output: A full-screen viewer component using the app-local Reveal feature bundle with direct modelId/revisionId (Pattern B), accessible at /viewer, with correct provider nesting and Vite config.

assertions.text: Reveal3DResources receives a resources array containing modelId 206509079235820 and revisionId 576781257263693
The resources array is memoized with useMemo
onModelsLoaded is wrapped in useCallback
CacheProvider and RevealKeepAlive are at the viewer page level, not co-located with RevealProvider in the same conditional block
The viewer container is full-screen (height: 100vh or h-screen or equivalent)
A route is configured at /viewer
vite.config.ts has worker: { format: 'es' } and uses manual util/assert/process aliases (NOT vite-plugin-node-polyfills)
main.tsx uses DuneAuthProvider from @cognite/dune as the auth provider (NOT CDFAuthenticationProvider)
The canvas content is lazy-loaded with React.lazy and Suspense
